Title: Navigate the OpenMRS Home dashboard apps grid using only the keyboard
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician on the OpenMRS Reference Application Home page; mouse physically disconnected or unused
Test_Steps: 1. Press Tab from the top of the page | 2. Continue pressing Tab to move across header, search, and app tiles | 3. Press Enter on the Active Visits app tile | 4. Use Shift+Tab to move backward
Expected_Result: Every interactive element (links, search, app tiles) receives focus in a logical order, each focused element is operable with Enter/Space, and activating the Active Visits tile opens that page without requiring a mouse

Title: Operate Patient Registration multi-step form using keyboard only
Preconditions: Logged in as Registration Clerk on the Register a Patient screen
Test_Steps: 1. Tab into the Given name field and type | 2. Tab to Family name and type | 3. Tab to Next/Continue control and press Enter | 4. Tab through gender radio group and select with arrow keys | 5. Tab to birthdate fields and enter values | 6. Tab to Confirm and press Enter
Expected_Result: All registration steps can be completed in sequence with keyboard only, radio groups respond to arrow keys, and the patient record is created without mouse interaction

Title: Verify no keyboard trap inside the patient search autocomplete dropdown
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient search widget present on the Find Patient Record page
Test_Steps: 1. Tab focus into the patient search input | 2. Type three characters to trigger the autocomplete list | 3. Press Down arrow to enter the suggestion list | 4. Attempt to Tab and Shift+Tab out of the suggestion list | 5. Press Escape
Test_Data: Search term=Tes; Suggestions appear for synthetic patients
Expected_Result: Focus can move into and out of the autocomplete list freely via Tab/Shift+Tab/Escape; focus is never trapped and the user can always reach the next control (WCAG 2.1.2)

Title: Verify no keyboard trap in modal dialogs (Start Visit / Delete confirmation)
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician on a patient dashboard with the Start Visit modal available
Test_Steps: 1. Open the Start Visit modal via keyboard | 2. Tab through all controls in the modal | 3. Confirm focus cycles within the modal and does not escape behind it | 4. Press Escape | 5. Confirm focus returns to the triggering control
Expected_Result: Focus is contained within the open modal while it is active (focus trap by design), Escape closes the modal, and focus returns to the element that opened it without becoming stranded

Title: Verify logical focus order matches visual reading order on the patient dashboard
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician viewing a patient dashboard with multiple widgets (visits, diagnoses, vitals)
Test_Steps: 1. Place focus at page start | 2. Tab through the page recording the order of focused elements | 3. Compare focus order with the left-to-right, top-to-bottom visual layout
Expected_Result: Focus order follows a meaningful sequence consistent with the visual/reading order; no focus jumps to off-screen or visually-later elements before earlier ones (WCAG 2.4.3)

Title: Verify a visible focus indicator is present on all interactive controls
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; default OpenMRS RefApp theme active
Test_Steps: 1. Tab through header links, buttons, form fields, and app tiles | 2. Observe each focused element for a visible focus ring or outline | 3. Note any control where the focus indicator is missing or hidden
Expected_Result: Every focusable control shows a clearly visible focus indicator with sufficient contrast against its background; no control receives focus invisibly (WCAG 2.4.7)

Title: Boundary: focus indicator remains visible against the dark header background
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; OpenMRS top navigation header rendered with its dark background
Test_Steps: 1. Tab to the Home link in the header | 2. Tab to the user account menu in the header | 3. Tab to the logout control | 4. Inspect focus ring contrast against the dark header at each stop
Expected_Result: The focus indicator on dark-header controls has a contrast ratio of at least 3:1 against adjacent colors and stays perceptible at every header control

Title: Verify a Skip to main content link is present and functional
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician on any OpenMRS RefApp page with repeated header navigation
Test_Steps: 1. Load the page and press Tab once from the very top | 2. Observe whether a Skip to main content link appears | 3. Activate it with Enter | 4. Press Tab again and confirm focus lands in main content
Expected_Result: A Skip to main content (bypass) link is the first focusable element, becomes visible on focus, and moves focus past the repeated navigation directly into main content (WCAG 2.4.1)

Title: Negative: absence of bypass mechanism on a page with large repeated navigation
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician on a page that lacks a skip link but has extensive repeated header/sidebar navigation
Test_Steps: 1. Load the page | 2. Tab from the top and count how many tab stops are required to reach main content | 3. Verify whether any bypass mechanism (skip link, landmark, heading nav) exists
Expected_Result: If no skip link, ARIA landmarks (main, navigation) or a heading structure must provide an alternate bypass; total absence of any bypass mechanism is a WCAG 2.4.1 failure to be reported

Title: Verify ARIA landmark roles partition the page (banner, navigation, main, contentinfo)
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; accessibility inspection tool (axe DevTools or browser a11y tree) available
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Inspect the accessibility tree for landmark roles | 3. Confirm banner, navigation, main, and contentinfo (or equivalent HTML5 landmarks) are present and unique where required
Expected_Result: Page exposes correct, non-duplicated landmark roles allowing screen-reader users to jump between regions; main landmark wraps the primary content (WCAG 1.3.1)

Title: Verify form controls expose correct roles and states in the accessibility tree
Preconditions: Logged in as Registration Clerk on the Register a Patient screen; a11y tree inspector available
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect the gender radio group in the a11y tree | 2. Confirm role=radiogroup with radio children and aria-checked states | 3. Inspect the Unknown checkbox role and aria-checked | 4. Inspect Next button role
Expected_Result: Radio group exposes role radiogroup/radio with correct aria-checked, checkboxes expose checkbox role with state, and buttons expose button role; states update as values change (WCAG 4.1.2)

Title: Verify every visible registration field has a programmatically associated label
Preconditions: Logged in as Registration Clerk on the Register a Patient demographics step
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect each input (Given name, Middle name, Family name) in the a11y tree | 2. Confirm each has an accessible name from a label, aria-label, or aria-labelledby | 3. Click each label text and confirm focus moves to its field
Expected_Result: Every form field has a programmatically determinable label; clicking a label focuses its control; no field is announced as unlabeled (WCAG 1.3.1, 3.3.2, 4.1.2)

Title: Negative: placeholder-only field with no persistent label is flagged
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician on a form where a field uses only placeholder text as its visible cue
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect the field in the a11y tree | 2. Check whether an accessible name exists when the placeholder is cleared | 3. Enter a value and confirm the label/name remains available
Expected_Result: A field relying solely on placeholder text (which disappears on input and is not a label) must be reported as a labeling failure; an accessible name must persist independent of placeholder (WCAG 3.3.2)

Title: Verify validation errors are programmatically associated with their fields
Preconditions: Logged in as Registration Clerk on the Register a Patient screen
Test_Steps: 1. Leave the required Given name field empty | 2. Attempt to proceed to the next step | 3. Inspect the error message element | 4. Confirm the field references the error via aria-describedby and is marked aria-invalid
Expected_Result: On validation failure the field is marked aria-invalid=true and links its error message via aria-describedby so screen readers announce the specific error when the field is focused (WCAG 3.3.1, 3.3.3)

Title: Screen reader announces error summary when registration submission fails
Preconditions: Logged in as Registration Clerk; NVDA or VoiceOver running on the Register a Patient screen
Test_Steps: 1. Submit the form with multiple required fields empty | 2. Listen for the error summary announcement | 3. Navigate to the summary and follow a link to the first errored field
Test_Data: Empty fields=Given name, Family name, DOB
Expected_Result: An error summary region (role=alert or aria-live=assertive) is announced automatically on failed submit, and each listed error links to its field (WCAG 3.3.1, 4.1.3)

Title: Verify screen reader announces dynamic Active Visits updates via live region
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; NVDA running; Active Visits list visible and updating
Test_Steps: 1. Open the Active Visits page with a screen reader active | 2. Have a synthetic patient checked in by a second clerk | 3. Listen for an announcement when the list updates
Expected_Result: The newly added active visit is announced through an aria-live polite region without the user needing to refresh or move focus (WCAG 4.1.3)

Title: Verify screen reader announces successful patient creation confirmation
Preconditions: Logged in as Registration Clerk; VoiceOver active; completing patient registration
Test_Steps: 1. Complete all registration steps with valid data | 2. Confirm the final step | 3. Listen for the success confirmation announcement
Expected_Result: The Created Patient Record confirmation is conveyed to assistive technology via a status/alert live region so non-visual users know the action succeeded (WCAG 4.1.3)

Title: Verify body text meets 4.5:1 contrast against its background
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; contrast analysis tool (axe or Colour Contrast Analyser) available
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Sample foreground and background colors of body text in vitals and diagnoses widgets | 3. Compute contrast ratios
Expected_Result: All normal-size body text has a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1 against its background (WCAG 1.4.3); any value below the threshold is reported as a failure

Title: Boundary: large text (>=18.66px bold / 24px) meets 3:1 threshold exactly
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; contrast tool available; page contains heading-size text
Test_Steps: 1. Identify large-text headings (patient name banner, section headings) | 2. Sample foreground/background colors | 3. Compute ratios and compare against the 3:1 large-text threshold
Expected_Result: Large-scale text meets at least 3:1 contrast; values at exactly 3:1 pass and values just below (e.g. 2.9:1) are flagged as boundary failures (WCAG 1.4.3)

Title: Verify UI component and graphical object contrast meets 3:1 (icons, borders, focus)
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; contrast tool available
Test_Steps: 1. Sample the contrast of form field borders against the page background | 2. Sample required-field indicator icons | 3. Sample the active state of toggles/checkboxes
Test_Data: Components=field borders, checkbox outline, required marker
Expected_Result: Active UI components and meaningful graphical objects have at least 3:1 contrast against adjacent colors so their boundaries/state are perceivable (WCAG 1.4.11)

Title: Negative: required-field and error states must not rely on color alone
Preconditions: Logged in as Registration Clerk; simulate grayscale/color-blindness view
Test_Steps: 1. View the registration form in grayscale | 2. Identify required fields | 3. Trigger validation errors | 4. Confirm required status and error status are conveyed by text/icon/asterisk, not color only
Expected_Result: Required and error states are distinguishable without color (asterisk, icon, or text label present); information conveyed by color alone is a WCAG 1.4.1 failure

Title: Verify the Logout icon control has an accessible name
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; a11y tree inspector and screen reader available
Test_Steps: 1. Locate the logout icon control in the header/account menu | 2. Inspect its accessible name in the a11y tree | 3. Navigate to it with a screen reader and listen to the announcement
Expected_Result: The logout icon button exposes a clear accessible name such as Log out (via aria-label or visually hidden text) so it is announced meaningfully, not as button or blank (WCAG 4.1.2, 2.4.4)

Title: Verify navigation/sidebar toggle icon button exposes name and expanded state
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician on a page with a collapsible navigation toggle
Test_Steps: 1. Locate the menu/sidebar toggle icon button | 2. Inspect accessible name and aria-expanded | 3. Activate it and confirm aria-expanded toggles true/false
Expected_Result: The toggle has an accessible name (e.g. Toggle navigation) and exposes aria-expanded reflecting the current open/closed state, updating on activation (WCAG 4.1.2)

Title: Negative: icon-only edit/delete buttons in a list lack distinguishing accessible names
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician viewing a list with repeated row-level icon actions (edit, delete)
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect the edit and delete icon buttons across multiple rows | 2. Confirm each has a unique, descriptive accessible name including row context
Test_Data: List=Allergies list with edit/delete per row
Expected_Result: Each row-level icon button has a distinct accessible name (e.g. Delete allergy Penicillin) so screen-reader users can distinguish identical icons across rows; generic or empty names are reported as failures (WCAG 2.4.4, 4.1.2)

Title: Verify data tables use proper header cells and scope associations
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician viewing a tabular widget (e.g. lab results or visit history)
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect the table markup/a11y tree | 2. Confirm column and row headers use th with appropriate scope | 3. Navigate cells with a screen reader and confirm headers are announced
Expected_Result: Table column/row headers are marked as header cells with correct scope so each data cell is announced with its associated header context (WCAG 1.3.1)

Title: Negative: layout achieved with table markup but missing header semantics
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician viewing a grid that visually looks like a table
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect the grid markup | 2. Determine if it is a data table lacking th headers or a layout table misusing semantics | 3. Verify screen-reader navigation does not announce phantom or missing headers
Expected_Result: A data table without header semantics (or a layout table exposing table semantics) is flagged; data tables must declare headers and layout structures must not falsely expose table roles (WCAG 1.3.1)

Title: Verify content reflows without horizontal scrolling at 400% zoom (320px width)
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician on the patient dashboard at 1280px base width
Test_Steps: 1. Set browser zoom to 400% (equivalent to 320 CSS px viewport) | 2. Review the patient dashboard | 3. Scroll and confirm no loss of content or two-dimensional scrolling for primary content
Expected_Result: Content reflows into a single column with only vertical scrolling; no content or functionality is lost and no horizontal scrolling is required for blocks of text (WCAG 1.4.10)

Title: Verify text remains readable and functional at 200% zoom
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician on the Register a Patient screen at default zoom
Test_Steps: 1. Set browser zoom to 200% | 2. Review form labels, fields, and buttons | 3. Complete a registration step at this zoom level
Expected_Result: At 200% zoom all text is readable, controls remain operable and do not overlap or clip, and the registration step can be completed (WCAG 1.4.4)

Title: Verify content adapts when user text-spacing overrides are applied
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; a text-spacing bookmarklet/extension applying WCAG 1.4.12 metrics
Test_Steps: 1. Apply line-height 1.5x, paragraph spacing 2x, letter spacing 0.12em, word spacing 0.16em | 2. Review the patient dashboard | 3. Confirm no clipped or overlapping text or lost content
Expected_Result: With user text-spacing applied, no content is clipped, truncated, or overlapped and all information remains available (WCAG 1.4.12)

Title: Verify session timeout warning is announced and extendable before logout
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician with an idle-session timeout configured in the OpenMRS RefApp
Test_Steps: 1. Remain idle until the pre-timeout warning appears | 2. Confirm a screen reader announces the warning | 3. Use the keyboard to extend the session before expiry
Test_Data: Idle timeout=configured; warning lead time present
Expected_Result: Before the session expires the user receives a perceivable, screen-reader-announced warning and can extend the time limit via keyboard, satisfying time-limit accommodation (WCAG 2.2.1)

Title: Negative: session expires without warning while clinician completes a form
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician entering a long encounter form; short idle timeout configured
Test_Steps: 1. Begin filling a long encounter/observation form | 2. Pause long enough to approach the timeout | 3. Observe whether any warning precedes data loss
Test_Data: Form=Encounter note; idle pause near timeout
Expected_Result: A timeout that silently discards entered clinical data without warning or recovery is a WCAG 2.2.1 failure and a patient-safety/data-integrity risk; the system must warn and preserve data

Title: Validate Start Visit modal focus state transitions (closed->open->closed)
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician on a patient dashboard; Start Visit control available
Test_Steps: 1. State Closed: focus on Start Visit trigger | 2. Activate to transition to Open: focus moves into modal, background inert | 3. Confirm focus trapped while Open | 4. Press Escape to transition to Closed | 5. Confirm focus returns to trigger
Expected_Result: Each state transition manages focus correctly: opening moves focus into the dialog and makes background inert, closing returns focus to the trigger; no orphaned focus in any state (WCAG 2.4.3, 4.1.2)

Title: Validate aria-expanded transitions on the account dropdown menu
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; account/user menu in header
Test_Steps: 1. State Collapsed: inspect aria-expanded=false | 2. Activate menu via keyboard: aria-expanded=true and focus enters menu | 3. Arrow through items | 4. Escape: aria-expanded=false and focus returns to trigger
Expected_Result: aria-expanded accurately reflects collapsed/expanded states across transitions, menu items are reachable by keyboard, and Escape restores both state and focus (WCAG 4.1.2)

Title: Decision table for form-field accessibility name resolution sources
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; a11y tree inspector available; test fields configured with varying labeling
Test_Steps: 1. Field with label only -> expect name from label | 2. Field with aria-label only -> name from aria-label | 3. Field with aria-labelledby -> name from referenced element | 4. Field with label + aria-label -> aria-label wins | 5. Field with none -> no accessible name (fail)
Test_Data: Variants: label-only; aria-label-only; aria-labelledby; label+aria-label; none
Expected_Result: Accessible name is computed per the ARIA name-computation precedence for each combination; the none case yields an empty name and is reported as a labeling failure (WCAG 4.1.2)

Title: Decision table for contrast pass/fail across text size and weight
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; contrast tool; sample texts of varied size/weight
Test_Steps: 1. Normal text 4.5:1 -> pass | 2. Normal text 4.4:1 -> fail | 3. Large text 3.0:1 -> pass | 4. Large text 2.9:1 -> fail | 5. Bold 14pt (large) at 3.1:1 -> pass
Expected_Result: Each size/weight/ratio combination is classified pass or fail strictly per WCAG 1.4.3 thresholds; boundary values just under threshold fail and at threshold pass

Title: Pairwise screen-reader x browser coverage for registration flow announcements
Preconditions: Test matrix of assistive tech and browsers prepared; synthetic registration data ready
Test_Steps: 1. NVDA + Chrome: complete registration, verify announcements | 2. JAWS + Edge: repeat | 3. VoiceOver + Safari: repeat | 4. NVDA + Firefox: repeat | 5. Compare label, error, and success announcements across pairs
Expected_Result: Field labels, validation errors, and success confirmations are announced correctly and consistently across each AT/browser pair; any pair-specific gap is documented (WCAG 4.1.2, 4.1.3)

Title: Pairwise OS x zoom x input-method coverage for dashboard operability
Preconditions: Multiple OS/zoom/input combinations available
Test_Steps: 1. Windows + 200% zoom + keyboard | 2. macOS + 400% zoom + keyboard | 3. Windows + 100% + switch device | 4. macOS + 200% + keyboard | 5. Verify operability and reflow in each
Test_Data: Pairs across OS, zoom, input method
Expected_Result: Dashboard remains operable and content reflows correctly across the sampled OS/zoom/input combinations with no loss of functionality (WCAG 1.4.10, 2.1.1)

Title: Exploratory keyboard-only session across core clinician workflows
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; mouse unplugged; 60-minute timebox
Test_Steps: 1. Without a mouse, attempt find patient, start visit, record vitals, add diagnosis, and logout | 2. Note any control unreachable or non-operable by keyboard | 3. Record focus, trap, or announcement issues encountered
Test_Data: Workflows: search, visit, vitals, diagnosis, logout
Expected_Result: All core clinician tasks are completable using the keyboard alone; any blocking interaction is logged as a defect with WCAG mapping (WCAG 2.1.1)

Title: Exploratory screen-reader walkthrough of patient dashboard semantics
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; NVDA active; 45-minute timebox
Test_Steps: 1. Using virtual cursor, traverse headings, landmarks, lists, and tables | 2. Confirm heading hierarchy is logical | 3. Note any unlabeled controls, redundant announcements, or missing structure
Expected_Result: Heading and landmark structure supports efficient screen-reader navigation; semantic gaps and verbose or missing announcements are documented (WCAG 1.3.1, 2.4.6)

Title: Verify heading levels are properly nested without skips
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; a11y tree or HeadingsMap tool available
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Extract the heading outline | 3. Confirm a single h1 and no skipped levels (e.g. h2 to h4)
Expected_Result: Headings form a logical hierarchy with one top-level h1 and no skipped levels, enabling structural navigation (WCAG 1.3.1, 2.4.6)

Title: Verify each page has a unique, descriptive document title
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician navigating between Home, Registration, and Patient Dashboard
Test_Steps: 1. Visit each page | 2. Read the browser tab/document title | 3. Confirm it describes the page and patient context where relevant
Expected_Result: Each page has a unique, descriptive title that identifies its purpose (and patient context where applicable) for orientation (WCAG 2.4.2)

Title: Verify the page declares a valid language attribute
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; ability to inspect the html element
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect the html element on multiple pages | 2. Confirm a valid lang attribute is set | 3. If locale is switched, confirm lang updates accordingly
Expected_Result: The page programmatically declares its human language via a valid lang attribute so assistive tech uses correct pronunciation; it reflects the active locale (WCAG 3.1.1)

Title: Verify informative images have appropriate alt text and decorative images are hidden
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; a11y tree inspector
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect the OpenMRS logo and any informative icons | 2. Confirm informative images expose meaningful alt text | 3. Confirm purely decorative images use empty alt or are hidden from AT
Expected_Result: Informative images convey their meaning through accessible text and decorative images are hidden from assistive technology to avoid noise (WCAG 1.1.1)

Title: Verify interactive targets meet minimum size and spacing for touch use
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician on a touch device or with target-size inspection
Test_Steps: 1. Measure tap targets of row-level icon buttons | 2. Confirm each meets the recommended minimum size and spacing | 3. Attempt activation on touch without mis-taps
Expected_Result: Interactive targets are large enough and spaced to be reliably activated by touch, reducing mis-activation in clinical use (WCAG 2.5.5/2.5.8 best practice)

Title: Verify the OpenMRS login form is fully keyboard and screen-reader accessible
Preconditions: On the OpenMRS RefApp login page; not yet authenticated; screen reader available
Test_Steps: 1. Tab to Username, type | 2. Tab to Password, type | 3. Tab to session Location select and choose | 4. Tab to Login and press Enter | 5. Confirm labels and any error are announced
Expected_Result: Login fields have associated labels, are reachable and operable by keyboard, errors are announced, and login completes without a mouse (WCAG 1.3.1, 2.1.1, 3.3.2)

Title: Negative: login error announced and does not leak which credential was wrong
Preconditions: On the OpenMRS login page; screen reader active
Test_Steps: 1. Enter an invalid username/password combination | 2. Submit | 3. Listen for the error announcement | 4. Read the error text
Expected_Result: The authentication error is announced via a live region and uses a generic message (does not reveal whether username or password was incorrect), balancing accessibility with security (WCAG 4.1.3)

Title: Boundary: accessible-name announcement for very long patient name in banner
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient with a maximal-length name registered; screen reader active
Test_Steps: 1. Open the dashboard of the patient with a long name | 2. Navigate to the patient name banner with a screen reader | 3. Confirm the full name is announced and visually not truncated in a way that loses information
Expected_Result: The full patient name is programmatically available and announced even at maximum length; any visual truncation still exposes the complete name to AT (WCAG 1.3.1)

Title: Verify FHIR Patient.name rendering preserves accessible structure for assistive tech
Preconditions: Resource Adapter Layer fetching Patient from a FHIR R4 endpoint; rendered in the patient banner
Test_Steps: 1. Retrieve a FHIR Patient resource | 2. Render name from name.given and name.family | 3. Inspect the rendered banner accessible name | 4. Confirm given+family compose a single coherent announced name
Expected_Result: FHIR name parts are composed into a coherent accessible name in the UI so screen-reader users hear the full name; no part is dropped or announced out of order (WCAG 1.3.1; FHIR R4)

Title: Verify coded clinical data (CodeableConcept) displays human-readable text, not raw codes
Preconditions: Resource Adapter Layer rendering a Condition/Observation with CodeableConcept; screen reader active
Test_Steps: 1. Retrieve a Condition with a coded diagnosis | 2. Render it in the diagnoses widget | 3. Confirm the display/text is shown and announced rather than the raw code | 4. Verify the code system is not read aloud to the user
Expected_Result: The human-readable display text of coded concepts is presented and announced; raw codes and system URIs are not surfaced to end users as the primary content (WCAG 1.3.1; FHIR R4)

Title: Verify HL7 v2-sourced patient data renders with accessible labels in the adapter UI
Preconditions: Resource Adapter Layer ingesting an HL7 v2 ADT message mapped to the patient banner
Test_Steps: 1. Ingest an ADT^A01 message with PID name fields | 2. Map PID-5 to the displayed name | 3. Inspect the rendered field for an associated label and accessible name
Expected_Result: Patient data sourced from HL7 v2 segments is displayed with proper labels and accessible names in the UI, identical to native data, so assistive tech announces it correctly (WCAG 1.3.1; HL7 v2)

Title: Security: focus is moved to logout confirmation and session cannot be operated post-logout via stale focus
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; screen reader active
Test_Steps: 1. Activate logout via keyboard | 2. Confirm focus moves to the login page or confirmation | 3. Press Back and attempt to operate a previously focused PHI control | 4. Confirm protected content is not operable without re-auth
Expected_Result: After logout focus moves to a safe post-logout context and previously focused PHI controls are not operable from cached pages without re-authentication (WCAG 2.4.3; security)

Title: API: REST patient response includes display fields needed to build accessible names
Preconditions: Authorized API client with access to the OpenMRS REST patient endpoint
Test_Steps: 1. Call the patient REST endpoint for a synthetic patient | 2. Inspect the JSON for display/preferredName fields | 3. Confirm fields needed to construct an accessible UI name are present and non-empty
Expected_Result: The API returns the display/name fields the UI needs to compose an accessible, human-readable name; missing display data that would force code-only rendering is reported (supports WCAG 1.3.1)

Title: Verify auto-updating content can be paused and respects reduced-motion preference
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; Active Visits auto-refreshing; OS prefers-reduced-motion enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Enable prefers-reduced-motion at the OS level | 2. Open a page with auto-updating or animated content | 3. Confirm non-essential motion is reduced/stopped and updates can be paused
Expected_Result: Non-essential animation/auto-update respects the reduced-motion preference and offers a pause/stop mechanism, preventing distraction and vestibular issues (WCAG 2.2.2, 2.3.3)

Title: Pairwise: accessible-name parity for patient banner across source systems
Preconditions: Resource Adapter Layer configured against OpenMRS, OpenEMR, HAPI FHIR, and omiiCARE backends
Test_Steps: 1. Render the patient banner from each backend | 2. Inspect the accessible name in each | 3. Compare label association and announced name across the four sources
Expected_Result: The patient banner exposes an equivalent, correctly labeled accessible name regardless of source system; any backend producing an unlabeled or inconsistent name is flagged (WCAG 1.3.1, 4.1.2)

Title: Verify navigation and component identification are consistent across pages
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician navigating multiple RefApp pages
Test_Steps: 1. Note the order and labels of header navigation on Home | 2. Navigate to Registration and Patient Dashboard | 3. Compare navigation order, labels, and icon meanings across pages
Expected_Result: Repeated navigation appears in a consistent relative order and components with the same function share consistent labels/icons across pages (WCAG 3.2.3, 3.2.4)

Title: Verify reversible confirmation for destructive clinical actions is keyboard/AT accessible
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; a deletable clinical item (allergy/diagnosis) present; screen reader active
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger delete on a clinical item via keyboard | 2. Confirm a confirmation dialog appears and is announced | 3. Cancel via Escape and confirm no deletion | 4. Repeat and confirm via keyboard
Expected_Result: Destructive clinical actions present an accessible, announced confirmation that is operable and cancelable by keyboard, preventing accidental data loss (WCAG 3.3.4, 2.1.1)

Title: Database: stored accessibility-relevant display name is non-null and trimmed for affected patients
Preconditions: Read access to a non-production OpenMRS database with synthetic data
Test_Steps: 1. Query person_name for synthetic patients | 2. Confirm given_name and family_name are non-null and non-empty | 3. Verify no leading/trailing whitespace that would distort announced names
Expected_Result: Stored name fields used to build accessible UI names are non-null, non-empty, and trimmed so rendered accessible names are correct; anomalies are reported as data-integrity issues (supports WCAG 1.3.1)

Title: Verify accessibility-affecting setting changes (locale/theme) are audit-logged
Preconditions: Logged in as Admin with audit logging enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Change the system or user locale affecting lang/text direction | 2. Save | 3. Inspect the audit log for the change record with user, timestamp, old/new value
Expected_Result: Changes to accessibility-affecting configuration are recorded in the audit trail with actor and before/after values for regulatory traceability (Audit; supports WCAG 3.1.1)
